Welcome to our first bonus close, which is the risk mitigator. So right off of that, risk mitigation is a function of revealing the true, non-exaggerated risks and stakes in not acting, and contrasting that with the safety in that risk reversal and your guarantee. So the result between those two dynamics should be a convincing final appeal that attunes your prospect to the greater risk in action. It's really when it boils down to the most confronting of our set of closes, so it does require a bit of finesse. So I like to take the edge off this by using the language instead of you language, which you'll later see when we break down an example. The other thing though in using day language is it creates an extra source of exclusive empowerment. You know, they are the people who are going to incur X, Y, and Z negative outcomes through inaction, but you, since you're consuming this offer, since you're reading this, since you have this opportunity, you don't have to have that same fate. So that does create a little bit of extra exclusivity and a source of exclusive empowerment to separate yourself from they, you know, they who are going to continue to suffer that fate that you no longer have to. General way of writing this, I like to write a headline that says X percent of immediate identifier will say no to this offer. Here are X number of ways they'll experience pain points. And then you'll list out the realistic stakes and the true associated cost of each one. And you will close with the guarantee to make taking action the safer bet. So here's how it comes into play for our friends at Soul Powered. Truth 75% of entrepreneurs, executives, and business leaders reading this will mull it over, bury this page on a Chrome tab and forget all about it as the enrollment period closes. Here are three ways they'll continue to flirt with burnout, install momentum as team members underperform, jump ship and leave them to plug the gaps with even more hustle. So once again, it's they language, it's those 75% of people and we're leading with the ways they're going to experience those moments of highest tension. So one, they'll miss seeing the critical talent and functional gaps needed to spur and sustain growth resulting in missed opportunities, straight jacketed momentum and mornings spent cuddled up to an iPhone putting out one fire after another. True cost, a leader's well-being, sanity and ability to make her highest contribution. Two, they'll know they need to hire to scale but they won't know who to hire and when to bring them on to guarantee a smarter ROI with each new team member. True cost, upwards of six figures per year. Three, if they luck out and hire for the right position at the right time, they'll experience critical gaps in their onboarding process that caps performance and creates a crippling disconnect between their hires potential and ability to realize it. True cost, your company's speed and capacity for sustained growth. And then we bring in that risk reversal. And with 30 risk-free days to work the system, when do you rather see what it's like to become indestructible? Isn't it time you worked on your business instead of in it? You've achieved some pretty amazing things to get to where you are right now, but you're also smart enough to know that what got you here won't get you there. This is your invitation to partner with us for the next 12 weeks to design your team for scale and power them to generate quicker, more predictable ROI and lead them towards rapid scale and record breaking performance. So with all that said, I invite you, even if it feels a little uncomfortable at first, to at least try writing a risk mitigator close and using it to close your sales page. And if you want another alternative and something that I like alternating in and out as a close, meet me in the next lesson where you will be reintroduced to the minimum viable commitment and quickest, most valuable win challenge. I'll see you in the next lesson.
